key_insight: A dynamical phase-field model with an electronic polarization field links ferroelectric domain structure directly to local optical response, enabling spatially resolved prediction of refractive index and electro-optic behavior.
engineering_relevance: This gives a computational design tool for ferroelectric photonic devices by showing how domains, temperature, and electric field can be tuned to engineer large local electro-optic coefficients.
method: Couples ferroelectric order to local optics through an added electronic polarization field.
Uses dynamical phase-field simulation to resolve wavelength- and temperature-dependent optical properties in complex microstructures.
Applies the model to BaTiO3 thin films under varying electric fields and temperatures.
evidence_signal: The model reproduces measured electro-optic coefficients in BaTiO3-on-silicon films and predicts >4000 pm/V near domain walls, compared with a bulk value of r51 = 1300 pm/V.
watch_item: Predictions depend on accurate phase-field parameters and material-specific optical coupling, so generalization to other ferroelectrics will need careful calibration.
paper_title: A Dynamical Phase-Field Model for the Optical Properties of Ferroelectrics
